title: Maintenance, Commit Graphs, Index Acceleration, and Bundles · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs maintenance, commit graphs, index acceleration, and bundles from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for measured maintenance, acceleration, and backup schedule after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.
Configure incremental-repack, loose-objects, commit-graph, prefetch, gc, worktree, rerere-gc, and scheduler strategy from measured need.
Explain commit-graph generations, changed-path Bloom filters, split chains, multi-pack indexes, bitmaps, and compatibility or invalidation boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G95 is a review in a disposable repository. measured history walks and pack fanout justify two incremental tasks without immediate pruning. The learner must predict commit graph and pack layout update while reachable objects and active writers remain protected, run git maintenance run --task=commit-graph --task=incremental-repack, explain history latency drops and connectivity remains valid with no unreachable-object deletion, test this changed condition: introduce a concurrent writer and defer destructive expiration while retaining maintenance logs, and preserve this recovery boundary: verify a fresh bundle restore and preserve previous pack indexes before manual repair.

Which action demonstrates Gita Gc Repack Prune Safety without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain gc, repack, cruft packs, prune, expiration, concurrent writers, keep files, geometric repack, and why manual cleanup needs reachability proof.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: git gc only compresses reachable data and can never expire recovery objects or race another process.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation.
